auth.scheme: DD-API-KEY and DD-APPLICATION-KEY headers on every request; keys never go in the query string
auth.redaction: keys must never appear in results, error messages, or logs

cancel.success: 204 with NO body — nothing to parse
cancel.not_found: 404 with an errors envelope; a canceler treats it as already gone (idempotent cancel)

create_attributes.scope: common search syntax string, e.g. env:prod AND service:checkout
create_attributes.monitor_identifier: strict union: {monitor_id: int64} XOR {monitor_tags: [string]} — never both keys
create_attributes.schedule_one_time: {start?, end?}: ISO-8601 datetimes that MUST include a zero UTC offset; omitted start = starts immediately, omitted end = continues forever (omit the schedule object entirely for start-now-forever)
create_attributes.schedule_recurring: {recurrences: [{rrule, duration, start?}], timezone?}: rrule is an RRULE string, duration is an integer plus one of m/h/d/w (e.g. 2h), start is ISO-8601 WITHOUT any UTC offset
create_attributes.omission: unset optional attributes are omitted from the document, never sent as null

relationships.monitor: {data: {id: string, type: 'monitors'}} for monitor_id downtimes; {data: null} for tag-scoped downtimes

errors.partial_failure: each downtime in a batch gets its own outcome; a failed create or cancel must not lose earlier successes or stop later items

test_fixture_notes.schedule_result: {created: [{name, id, scope, status, monitorId (string|null), createdBy}], failed: [{name, status (number|null when rejected client-side), errors}]}
test_fixture_notes.cancel_result: 'canceled' | 'already_gone'; batch form {canceled: [], alreadyGone: [], failed: [{id, status, errors}]}
